methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $385M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $2.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Divergent
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Agility Robotics, a Series E robotics and manufacturing company founded by Damion Shelton and Jonathan Hurst, has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 private robotics companies and 1 public comparable, positioned within the same sector and at equivalent or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $385M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, implying a $539M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), and Locus Robotics ($2.0B, Series F)—establishes a median valuation of $2.0B with a range of $2.0B to $2.3B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $458M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The material divergence between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty around whether Agility's metrics, growth trajectory, and market positioning align with later-stage, higher-valued peers or warrant a more conservative premium to deployed capital. Resolution of this gap depends on visibility into revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and unit economics relative to the peer cohort.
